title: oeceoclades decaryana
intro: Oeceoclades decaryana, also known as the 'Madagascar orchid', is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in African medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Oeceoclades decaryana include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat various health ailments, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
